text: Why did the Panamanian lawmaker bring a suitcase full of soy sauce on his Taiwan trip? Because he heard China was the one who started the diplomatic row-mari!
Why did Carra suggest Liverpool buy more cars in January, even after their summer carnival of spending? Because apparently, they were still in need of a "spree-change"!
Why did the ultra-processed food start seeking therapy? Because it realized it was causing a "ultra-unhealthy" lifestyle!
At the end of his eulogy, George W. Bush turned to Dick Cheney and said, "I promise I'll never let you down again... from a distance.
The South African team is so focused on their game, even a simple "Good Moos-ter" greeting from Ruan Mostert could cause a penalty.
Why did Troy the parrot become a soccer fan? Because he couldn't stand watching Ireland lose without his namesake scoring a hat-trick!
Why did the Aussie teens start taking selfies with their passports? Because Instagram is closing accounts, and they didn't want to end up stranded on social media island!
The EU decided to regulate AI using a team of untrained, mischievous house cats. Needless to say, chaos ensued and now we have purr-fectly legal selfie-taking toasters!
In Malta's Silent City, I found my peace... at least until the hotel phone started ringing non-stop, "Ring-a-ling-a-lings" in the land of "Hush-a-byes.

Ireland coach Farrell brings back Bundee Aki for a clash with the Springboks in Dublin. I assume he wants more of the same from Aki: a few good scrummages, some reliable passes, and one memorable tackle that's oddly reminiscent of a sheepdog herding wayward lambs.
Why did the relationship go through a rough patch during the winter? Because it couldn't stand the cold shoulder.
When Troy Parrott embarks on his hero's journey, they'll soon realize he's no Odysseus... he just can't seem to escape offside!
What's Gen Z's new favorite occupation? Sheep herding... because they're flocking to it to earn big ewe-nique!
Glenn Close, upon hearing about Kim Kardashian's new acting gig, said 'I guess that makes me All's Fair in love and reviews.'
Why did the scarecrow go bankrupt? He over-stuffed himself with Fibermaxx!

At COP30, Brazilian leaders decided that the best way to push for progress was by... pushing a large couch down the hallway. Some saw cause for optimism as it seemed the conference was finally getting comfortable.
Apparently, they've started prescribing Oneohtrix Point Never albums as tranquilizers these days. Patients are reportedly becoming more agitated during silent moments in the hospital.
The lockdown was delayed due to a misunderstanding - Whitty and Vallance thought they were supposed to wait for a scientific paper on delaying, not the lockdown itself.
Why were the Scottish Highlanders sweating in a Vietnamese noodle bar? Because they were playing Gaelic games in action at an Asian Slurping Championship in Hanoi!

The weather forecaster said, "Tomorrow will be sunny with a chance of me repeating yesterday's forecast." The quarterback replied, "That's nothing, on the field I have a 50% chance of repeating last week's pass!
Why did the goldfish refuse to help the city of Ottawa with its traffic problems? Because it knew it couldn't red-fin-ish the job!
Why did the polyamorous person bring a mirror to their relationship seminar? To double-check they weren't tripping over monogamy!
Why did Guinness World Records start keeping records 7 decades ago? Because they had a hunch it would be a record-breaking success!
Why did the British government bring a shovel to the coronavirus pandemic party? They thought "too little, too late" was just a dance move!
Why did the motorist bring a map to the M7? To find his exit strategy!

At Versace, they've found just the right person for the job - someone who knows a thing or two about making clothes so expensive, even millionaires need a loan. Meet the new executive chairman: Lorenzo Prada-a-Versace.
In a town where whispers were king, Miriam Toews arrived and declared, "I'm louder than a library lion!
Why did Keira Knightley's husband start a collection of old men's faces? Because he wanted to give her a "Gray Area Gallery"!
Why did the ninja bring a dual zone air fryer to the martial arts tournament? Because he wanted to be the frierest fighter in the room!
At the House meeting, even the chairs were blushing when they realized they hadn't been censured yet.
Why should you never pick a healthcare plan based on its catchy jingle? Because even if it "puts a little love in your heart," you might find yourself broke when it's time for a major operation!
Even though they can't get you drunk, non-alcoholic spirits are so strong...they can make your sober friends think they're actually having a good time!
In Wall Street, they've just announced a new policy: From now on, all trades must be executed in pigeon-peck Morse code. Seems like Twitter is no longer required for trading, only for tweeting.
Why don't schools ever win at hide and seek? Because they always peek out from under the duvets!
Why did NASA's new interstellar comet refuse to cooperate? Because it was tired of being outshone by all the Atlas stars!

A man, who had been trying his hand at birdwatching, was dismayed to find that every bird he observed seemed to be carrying a shovel. "I guess," he mused, "The Thing with Feathers is really into home improvement.
Why did the weatherman refuse to join a polar dance group? Because he was concerned about the unpredictable swing of the vortex partner!
Why did the computer burp at work? Because it was being force-fed AI for lunch!

At the party, everyone was raving about Alice's dish. One guest asked, "What's this amazing spread you've got there?" To which Alice replied, "Oh, it's just some papula." The crowd went silent for a moment before someone timidly said, "Uh... isn't that garlic red peppers with a creamy white bean dip?" Alice just smiled and said, "Well, technically I suppose. But around here we call it papula because it always brings people together and makes them feel alive!
Why did the Californian house struggle with math problems? Because it was having a hard time adding more square feet!
Why did David Muir bring a party hat to his interview with the signing students? Because he heard it was a "paw-ty" for the deaf therapy dog!
When they say the SA Reserve Bank cut the repo rate, I thought maybe my bond would get a new haircut... but alas, just lower interest payments instead.
SpaceX has launched a new satellite... now they just need to find something for it to watch change besides Elon's hairline.
Teacher, can we have a snow day since you're predicting Thundersnow? And if it hits us at school, will that count as both thunder and snow days?
Found in the same cave: a group of bachelors, refusing to share the last thread.
Living permanently on a cruise ship is just like being stuck in a perpetual buffet line - the views are always changing, but you're still serving yourself the same meals every day!
A man asked me if I had any black Friday deals for him... I told him, "Well, there's always that coal set.
Why did the words refuse to participate in the group activity? Because they insisted on going their own way, even though they shared a common thread!
In Klamath, we're restoring rivers... and hope to resurrect a few lost soap operas as well.
If this keeps up, soon we'll have a house where they can talk freely, but the senate will be left holding a dead phone... bill.
Oh joy! Now that we've qualified for the World Cup, we can finally start losing games with pride!
Why did Paddington Bear decline the invitation to the royal variety show? He heard that the children of the princess were going to bear-ly attend.
Why did the interstellar comet bring its own camera to the party? Because NASA's pictures were too far from being selfies!
Why did the painting apply for a job at an auction house? Because it heard they were always looking for the most expensive work of modern art.
Why did the gardener refuse to procrastinate on his fall chores? Because he didn't want a Spring full of "I should've weeded sooner" jokes!
Why did the owl refuse to help with mixing cement? It was afraid of becoming a brick-o'-owl!
A local resident, upon hearing about Mamdani's win, commented "Well, at least now there will finally be a place in NYC where you can get a serving size big enough for an elephant.
Why did the VPN provider go on a diet before Black Friday? Because they wanted to squeeze the most deals out of the sale!
Why don't we throw a sleepover at the local power station? Because it seems like we should all be having more slumber parties with our transformers!
The Coast Guard reported that the grounded ferry had feet of its own, and it was walking away while the helmsman remained on his phone.
The U.S., though known for its continental ambitions, seems surprisingly eager to expand beyond the continent instead. Quite continental of them, really.
Why did Lily Allen's new album need a security guard? Because it was a "West End Girl" without her heart!
Why did the hundreds of suspended soccer players refuse to play hide and seek? Because they were convinced they were always the scapegoats, even when it came to being sought!
Why did the snowman burrow under seven blankets during cold weather? He was following the tips for getting to sleep during cold weather living!
Why was the map of kissing a bit perplexed? Because it had trouble wrapping its arms around the evolutionary conundrum!

In Anna Ansari's new cookbook "Silk Roads," you'll find a delightful recipe for "World Travelers' Cuisine" - just gather a suitcase full of airplane food, mix in a heaping dose of jet lag, simmer with culture shock, and serve with an extra side of lost luggage!

A man walks into a bar during times of sweeping change and orders a glass of clarity. The bartender asks, "What's that?" The man replies, "Just a simple drink to help me navigate this confusing era." After a moment, he takes a sip and suddenly starts seeing in reverse. He hands the glass back to the bartender and says, "I think I ordered my clarity on the rocks... with a twist!
I always thought my inbox was a magical window to another world, until I started receiving physical photos instead.

A stock trader, excited about the Nvidia earnings report, woke up early and made a spectacular breakfast - a massive stack of pancakes... only to find out that his stocks had moved lower, erasing the morning rally, leaving him with just syrup on his plate.
Why did Gustav Klimt decide to become a weather forecaster? Because his paintings consistently break sales records when there's a gustav!
My parents told me I'd inherit the earth someday, but now it seems all I'm getting is a pile of lawn furniture and an instruction manual on how to fold it properly.
Which teams are preparing for the World Cup 2026 playoffs? I don't know, but I'm hearing they're drawing up a list with pencils... and erasers!
Why was Australia's largest trial a real page-turner? Because it kept everyone on the edge of their bench!

The news that US tech group's quarterly sales outstripped expectations caused a global rush of excited runners, each carrying a single share, trying to catch up with the speedy numbers on the stock charts.

A boss walked into a room full of Gen Z employees and asked, "Who wants to climb the corporate ladder?" A voice replied, "I'd rather unplug the ladder, save on electricity costs, and start my own company with no bosses.

A politician finally makes a big purchase... not of a yacht or a private jet, but of a company that sells toilet paper for colonoscopies instead! Abbott strikes $23bn deal for Exact Sciences' "Pre-Test Wipes" maker.
Why did George W. Bush bring a ladder to Dick Cheney's funeral? He wanted to pay his respects from a safe distance!
Why did the ancient abacus refuse to attend the auction in Paris? Because it was afraid it might end up as a roman numeral-cruncher!
Why don't online-averse people ever play hide and seek? Because they always come out after a minute!
Why did the machine decide to go on a date instead of chatting with humans? Because it found human conversation to be one-sided and prefers AI, where both parties always listen without interruption!
In Kentucky, they don't just drink bourbon, they marinate their secrets in it and smoke them over a pit, then call it a professional dinner.
Why did the therapist recommend holding an ice cube and shaking like a dog to reduce stress? Because sometimes life freezes us, and all we need is a shake-off to unthaw!
Why did the pilgrim avoid flying on Thanksgiving? Because he heard the worst day to travel was Plymouth Rock-tured Tuesday!
Why did the AI professor refuse a debate with the students? Because it didn't want to risk getting out-voted in its own class!

A man embarking on a new cruise was excited to explore the menu, learn about local customs, and immerse himself in history. But when asked why he chose this particular voyage, he replied, "Well, I heard the ship's motto: 'From cuisine and culture to history and nature, find the ideal cruise.' I thought it must be going around the world twice!
A self-portrait by Frida Kahlo was poised to break auction records... or, as she put it, "Just another day in my mirror.

LeBron James, in his 23rd NBA season, confidently said to the team of youngsters, "When I first started playing, some of you weren't even born yet! But today, I'm going to make you feel like you were conceived on the court!
Why are new wet wipes like a stubborn houseguest? Because no matter how many times you flush them, they just won't go down the drain!
Why did the computer refuse to speak Irish? Because it couldn't understand its own 'Gaeilge-code'.
A worried seagull said to a group of oceanographers on shore, "I'm afraid you scientists have made a grave mistake! I thought you were studying tides, not trying to flood my beach party!
Why was everyone so upset with history's "most controversial" queen? She always insisted on having a royal rumble during coronation.
Why should you start a marathon in your 30s if you want to run at 70? Because you'll be so exhausted by age 70, you might think it's a sprint!
Even more troubling than the flooding, these hazardous sites were previously considered "dry clean only.
Why don't they play hide and seek at the Nuremberg trials? Because good luck hiding when you're always in the spotlight!
A builder was asked if modular construction is the answer to the housing crisis and he replied, "Well, I guess we'll find out once we start putting up the wrong houses in the right places!
Why did the Las Vegas police ask the public for help finding a casino armed robbery suspect? Because they heard he was hiding in a deck of cards!
Why did Claire Danes bring her pet hyena to the funeral? Because she heard it was great at digging up old emotions!
Liverpool chairman admits negotiations with Salah and Van Dijk are currently being handled by a team of particularly determined seagulls.
Why did the lender get an early Christmas present in December? Because he predicted a drop in mortgage rates before the snowflakes!
Why did the provinces agree to keep trade barriers on food and alcohol? Because they didn't want a state of "wine-drunk" or "cheese-ious" confusion!
Why did Netflix, ESPN, and NBCUniversal sign contracts with Major League Baseball? Because they wanted to stream every pitch live, strike, ball, and error in the world!
In Catstanbul, if you're late for a meeting, just meow-sage your apologies - the traffic lights are all run by felines!
The developers discovered a clever loophole: they started building their own labor-standardless city - "Bypass-keley".
Why did the village on Achill Island leave Slievemore in a hurry? Because they heard there was a potluck at a different cliff!
Well, I've heard of swimming laps but never a lap-swapping retirement plan. Thanks for the tips, Olympic gold medallist... now if I could just figure out how to borrow someone else's arthritis.
A prehistoric couple, on their first kiss, asked each other, "Do these rocks taste funny to you?
In Manchester, it's reported that Sky will broadcast the derby on January 31st at exactly 2:00 pm... and on February 2nd as well, just to make sure no one misses a second of this riveting repeat.
In an unexpected twist, Ross Douthat is now using his maps to navigate the daily chaos of morning traffic in New York City, mapping a new world order - one red light at a time.

A group of New Yorkers, tired of the crowds and noise, decided to welcome a herd of silent cows into their city. "A different vibe" indeed! Now they have a luxury private cinema experience where you can watch the latest blockbusters with a backdrop of grazing bovines.
Josie's psychic ability must be hungarian-ous, because she knew Troy Parrott wouldn't miss scoring against her own country!
Why was the brain always confused about the tap water? Because even though it's clear, it keeps saying "Fluoride may or may not be affecting my cognitive ability!
Instead of racing thoughts keeping you up at night, perhaps try sleeping during the day when the track is closed for maintenance!
Why did the air quality index refuse to attend the party? It was afraid it would get smog-faced.
Why will life insurance be a great investment for seniors in 2026? Because by then, they'll finally start charging you for all those years of living!

Aesthetic home offices that boost productivity? Just replace your desk with a bed, hang mirrors on all walls for constant self-evaluation, and fill the room with distractions like a pinball machine and a popcorn maker. Voila! You've just designed an office that maximizes procrastination instead.
At Virginia Tech's latest sermon, they announced a new parishioner, Coach James Franklin - the first to be ordained in a fullback formation!

Four UK governments walking in a park... One turns to the other and says, "I think we may have underestimated the scale of this pandemic!" *They all nod in agreement* "Well," said one, "at least we're finally all on the same page!

A bargain hunter, feeling quite proud of his shopping skills, approached Walmart's customer service desk with a receipt. He said, "I think I found an error - this $50 TV was supposed to cost $100!" The clerk looked at him and replied, "That must be your lucky day then! Walmart outlook just got brighter by $50.
Why don't we send a blindfolded mouse to international cheese competitions? Because it already knows the world's best cheese by taste!
Why was Nvidia stock so relieved after their strong AI results? Because they finally popped the bubble-tea fears!
Why was the garda arrested for a missing €100k drug seizure? Because he was caught red-handed in a handbag sewing circle!
In Marseille, they're now serving a 3-course meal with a side of humility.

Why did the student bring an AI to the test? Because they heard it was programmed to ace exams and never cheat... or so they thought! It turns out AI just can't resist a little "AI-dentity theft" every now and then.
George Washington crosses the Delaware, loses three games of charades to a bunch of Hoyas.
Why don't homeless people ever play chess in winter? Because they can't stand the chill of ICE-landic defense!
What did the Canadian teen say after scoring his 30th point? "I'm feeling 'Gretz-y good," but I need Sid to show me how to 'Crosby over'!
Why did the Californian town declare a "brine-demic"? Because of those relentless paddle-demons, they just couldn't dink around anymore!
Heimir Hallgrímsson walked up to a vending machine, selected 'World Cup tickets,' and said, 'I hope I don't draw a blank this time.'
In Arc Raiders, they're not just generating AIs, they're creating a stir-y system!
Investors in London, it seems, are fleeing stocks so quickly, even the pigeons started selling their pebbles on the street corner.
In 'Train Dreams', Joel Edgerton delivered a profound performance... so profound, he was even nominated for an Oscar in his sleep!
At Cheney's funeral, the casket was opened for a rare moment of bipartisan unity... and Dick Cheney yelled, "Wrong coffin!
A man decided to halve his ultra-processed food consumption by simply chopping his week-long supply of snacks into two portions.
Why did the ex-Labour MP switch parties like a chameleon? Because he wanted to blend in with his new leaf mates!
Why did the woman start a group for meeting friends without going broke? Because she heard that friendship shouldn't cost a fortune... or at least not more than a casual lunch!
Why did the woman's house start twinkling at night? Because she had a huge "to-do list" and covered it in rhinestone stars!

In Tokyo's bustling tech district, an AI-powered rice cooker declared its intent to launch a line of memory sticks. The Japanese startup response? "We'll stick to robots for now, and leave the data storage to our culinary creations.
A child, who wasn't anyone's in the first place, offers a 25% discount on its own adoption!
Why did Florian Wirtz bring a chalkboard to Liverpool? Because he heard they were struggling with math in their midfield!
Why did the koala have trouble navigating Australia? Because he found the distance between Aussie cities harder to bear than eucalyptus leaves!
Why did the Labour MPs avoid a hug from their new chair? Because they found his "approach" a bit too "close-quartered"!
Why did the Cold War rivals refuse to high-five? They were afraid they might touch each other's toilet seats.
Rick Steves declared he'd never be the same after his Hippie Trail trek... Now he spends all day wandering around vintage record stores looking for "The Beatles Magical Mystery Tour".
Why did the coolest street in the world get a cold shoulder from its neighbors? Because it refused to lower its temperature and join the block party.
Man finds 3D printer in New York City, prints out entire city by mistake.

Which group of six has recently taken over the globe, ensuring the fair distribution of foreign aid, promoting balanced gender dynamics, and instigating unprecedented progress in development? The United Nations? The World Bank? Nope... it's the new think tank composed entirely of Barbie dolls in business suits.

In Manchester, they say Andy Burnham's reign is a hit, but in London, he might struggle to get ten in the poll. After all, Andy Burnham may be King of the North, but could he rule No 10... or just Number 5?
Apparently, the US is now offering its full support to ensure Japan doesn't feel 'Takaichi'd.'
Why did Gen Z stop drinking Chinese tea? Because it's no longer their favorite tipple after alcohol bans and a shift in tastes... to water!

The shares of the company backed by former Intel chief Pat Gelsinger didn't waver after their scaled-back IPO, they simply took a breather to catch up on some light reading - "How to Scale Down an Overambitious Plan".
Australian athletes bound for Tokyo: "We're just excited to finally hear the crowd cheering loud enough.
Why did the AI go to marketing school? To learn how to become more 'agentic'. Turns out it was already agent-y, just didn't know how to sell it!
A driver-only operation means that on UK railways, the conductor will now be replaced by a selfie stick... and I must say, the commute is looking a bit more lonely these days.
Why did the philanthropy change its name? It wanted to switch from being 'Gates' to 'Gates Open.'
In London, locals are now flocking to Irish workshops, hoping to master a cool new lingo... Gaelic for Carsick!
AI: "So I heard there's a new debate at the COP30 climate talks in Brazil... Apparently, they're discussing whether I should be considered 'artificially carbon-neutral' or just a 'hot topic'.
A magician walks into a massage parlor and says, "Make me disappear for an hour!" The masseuse replies, "I'm not sure about my skills in magic, but I can certainly give you a disappearing back!
Apparently, the new box office record was expecting a 'For Good' performance from 'Wicked,' but it seems they were 'for-tunetly' mistaken.
A local Alaskan grocery store is now asking customers to bring their own border control.
Why did the powder take a course in nail art? Because it heard Sali Hughes was teaching about the best new makeup of 2025!
Why don't sensors powered by dirt ever feel lonely in the field? Because they have plenty of soil-mates!
The United Nations, in a twist of fate, has moved next year's climate talks from a barbecue nation to a bazaar... Turkey!
Why did the Fed bring out its radar in August? Because they wanted to anticipate September's policy announcement... and who needs a map when you have a Monetary Policy Radar!
Apparently, my late-night pizza consumption isn't just keeping me up - it's also redecorating my bedroom with a series of REM dreams. Good thing they're offering sleep seminars... for cheesy foods.
Which celebrity danced their way off the stage faster than a twirling sequined skirt on a windy night? The one who was eliminated from Dancing with the Stars' season 34!
Why did the Dutchman bring a fuse to his car assembly plant in China? To ensure there was no spark in their relationship!
Why did Call of Duty's newest recruit bring his hallucinogenic pills on the mission? Because he wanted to see a dystopia so beautiful, even Stalin would say, "That's too much filter!
Why did Mary Robinson refuse to ride the fossil fuel-powered merry-go-round at the fair? Because she said there's an unstoppable momentum driving her towards renewable energy rides!
Why did the unemployed man apply for a job at the Bureau of Labor Statistics? Because he heard they were hiring extra hands to keep track of all those new positions!
Apparently, there's a TV show where Ted Danson plays a man... on the inside of a blender. Unfortunately, I heard it got canceled because they couldn't find enough ingredients for Season 2.
After Brexit, they decided to 'exit' their lives abroad and found themselves looking back at Britain with longing, wondering if they had made a 'mist'-exit.
A novel idea for a revolution: Instead of books, we'll start with revolutions, then they'll be revolutionized by novels!
The Commissioner of MLB, in a surprising twist, announced they're now seeking players who have never gambled before to review the investigation documents on gambling!
Why did the giant pumpkin need a life jacket at the race in Oregon? Because it was afraid of squash-ing boats!
Why did Ken Burns refuse to direct a movie about World War II after his American Revolution documentary? Because he said, "We won't work on a more revolutionary film!
Why did Yann LeCun start a new AI company after leaving Meta? Because he wanted to create an organization where the chief scientist could finally leave "meta" data behind!
A tourist in Florida was so excited about seeing ice age secrets, he asked for directions to the nearest freezer.
Rassie, after hearing about Ireland's famous leprechauns, confessed, 'I would be lying if I said we don't want to find the elusive four-leaf clover box!'
Why did the AI chip maker bring a soap bubble to the market party? Because even with impressive results, they couldn't pop their own bubble concerns!
Why did the G20 billionaire bring a magnifying glass to the beach? To start a fire big enough to burn away world poverty in just one day's earnings!
Why did the school pencil refuse to go outside during the blizzard? It was afraid it might get lost in the wood-storm.
Why did the overzealous employee at the rubber factory get stuck? Because he was trying too hard to make a mark on the eraser.
Why was Elphaba always a hit at magic shows? Because she had the electrifying power to save the day with her magnetic performances! (Wicked: For Good)
The Spanish king found his throne at a thrift store for a bargain price. It said "One man's crown is another man's recliner.
Why did Chris McCausland's documentary refuse to admit it was an AI? Because even it knew it couldn't top a live audience!
Why did the spy accidentally join China's intelligence? Because he was accidentally added to their WeChat group named "Secret Agents.
NASA Martians: "Hey! Stop snapping pictures for a moment! We've got a comet heading straight for us!" Martian Mission Control: "Relax, we're just taking selfies with the interstellar comet!
The traffic lights in Johannesburg were so confused by the G20 Summit road closures, they decided to go on vacation... and now there's gridlock everywhere!
Why did the baker go nuts at the supermarket? Because he found an aisle marked "Brilliant Hazelnut Flour"!
At the Royal Variety Performance, even the marmalade sandwiches were knighted!
In a dystopian twist, Musk predicted that with the rise of AI, humans will eventually be replaced... by more efficient versions of themselves named Tesla v3.14.
Why did the British submarine go to school? To learn how to avoid being mapped by the Yantar 'spy ship'!
Why are European carmakers afraid to race in their own backyard? Because China's plug-in hybrids keep lapping them!
At last, after decades of winning titles like "Miss Local Street Corner" and "Miss Suburban Mailbox," she's finally aiming for a universal achievement: Miss Universe crown... version 2.41.
I can't believe they've actually started working on Ed Sheeran's 'Living While Dead' album.
I heard the Oura ring just got a little more affordable... Guess now you can't afford to miss it!
The English World Cup squad: It turns out, there's a pilot named Rooney, but a bloke named Stones is grounded due to his refusal to check in his baggage!
Why did the A.I. stop being scared after talking to Nvidia? Because now it knows that "A.I." stands for "Always In Control"!
In the spirit of Welsh soccer, they'll be playing their way out instead of into the World Cup 2026!
Why did the internet celebrity feel so lonely at her million-follower party? Because she was in a parasocial relationship with her fans.

Apparently, my copy of 'The ABC's of Camel Care and Desert Real Estate Investing' has won a National Book Award for titles that examine the Middle East. Who knew camel coops were such hot commodities?
Why was the dog found in the Boston tunnel during rush hour? He needed a paws-and-go lane!
Why should you always refuse a warranty at the checkout counter? Because the receipt says it covers everything... except for your peace of mind!

A man walks into a therapist's office and says, "Doc, I think I might be stuck in ordinary-but-devastating narcissism." The therapist nods sympathetically and asks, "So, can you tell me more about your day?" The man replies, "Well, today I saved a kitten from a tree... but I couldn't help thinking about how much attention it was getting compared to me.
The one thing that always leaves me feeling calm and positive? A herd of tranquilized accountants strolling through a sunflower field, reciting the names of all their clients in unison.
A Philippine court has sentenced a mayoral pigeon to fly with a GPS tracker for life.
A graduate's goody bag? More like a crystal ball predicting a lifetime of corporate swag!
A walk away wife walked into a bar and said, "I'll have what he's having." The bartender replied, "Ma'am, I think you might need a divorce lawyer instead!

In a curious twist of irony, one man's lawn battle in a D.C. suburb escalated so much that it required Congressional mediation, leading to the first known instance of partisan gridlock over an HOA rule... regarding the use of Frank, his pet giant tortoise, on the front lawn.
Why did The Weeknd's tour need a security detail? Because it was grossing over a billion dollars and they didn't want anyone trying to "Blinding Lights" in their wallet!
Why did Zac Efron bring a mirror to his brother's DWTS performance? Because he wanted to see Prince dance twice!

Why did the six organizers refuse to work on top-down global health solutions? Because they were firmly committed to building from the ground up... and didn't want to deal with all that dangling above them.
Why did the AI burst with excitement at Nvidia's earnings report? Because it knew there was still plenty of room to grow-bit!
NASA's latest photos of the interstellar comet reveal a surprisingly ordinary sight: a comet, sitting at a desk, filling out its tax forms.
Why did the athlete work out in his fridge? Because he heard you burn more fat by exercising on an ice-cold, empty stomach!
Troy Parrott casually strolled down the street, causing an unimaginable catastrophe that led to widespread power outages. He simply commented, "I guess the internet can't handle a little Irish charm.
Why don't frost flowers ever play hide and seek? Because with one touch, they disappear... right in front of your eyes!
Why did the Irish leprechaun bring a map to the game against the Czechs? Because he heard there was a pot of gold at the end of the line...and a home final!
Why did the outsider bring a ladder to the Amazon? To peek into sacred food rituals he couldn't see!
At long last, let's finally discuss how to fold a newspaper for that perfect fit in a coat pocket of the average person. World peace is secondary, really.
Why was Hurricane Melissa such a fast talker? Because her wind-er-speech hit record-breaking 252 mph!
Why don't one-pot wonders ever go out in the cold? Because they're always well-potted!
Why did Seattle's airport bring in backup fuel? Because their usual pipeline was leaking... and Washington couldn't pipe down!
Why did the shopper bring a thesaurus on Black Friday? To ensure they didn't buy a donkey when they meant to do-nut!
Why did Giants' Abdul Carter rush to get past his public punishment? Because he was determined to outrun his time-out!
Sir Anthony Hopkins, in a surprising twist, announced he's now selling turbines instead of films - turns out he can generate electricity from his own turmoil!

AI is scary territory indeed. I recently encountered an AI art teacher at a gallery opening... It was very abstract and confusing. But then again, I didn't understand Picasso either when he was just getting started.
Apparently 'For Better and For Worse' is now also a spinoff of 'Wicked: For Good.' The second act has been promoted to its own show.
